items.title: Unauthenticated Arbitrary File Upload in Amasty Order Attributes
items.summary: Sansec discovered an unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes that allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec found a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, which can lead to remote code execution via a crafted cookie on any storefront request.

items.title: Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source Security Update
items.summary: Adobe released a security update resolving critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities, including arbitrary code execution, arbitrary file system write, application denial-of-service, and security feature bypass.

items.title: Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source Security Update
items.summary: Adobe released a security update addressing multiple critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to unauthorized access, remote code execution, and service disruption if left unpatched.

items.title: Unrestricted File Upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered the PolyShell vulnerability, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files disguised as images, with a fix included in Magento 2.4.9 as part of APSB25-94.

items.title: Critical Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released a critical security update to protect against multiple vulnerabilities that could allow attackers to bypass security controls, gain escalated privileges, execute unauthorized malicious code, and compromise store data.
